title: U-VISA PROGRAM - 211info
text: MULTNOMAH COUNTY COURTHOUSE U-VISA PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance, including providing information on eligibility criteria, connection with certification officials, and support and guidance in applying for the U-Visa program. This program allows eligible victims to temporarily stay in the United States and access necessary services while aiding law enforcment efforts. Victims have access to emergency medical care, access to police assistance, and community-based services geared towards increasing safety and well-being. 1200 SW 1st Avenue Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Individuals who are immigrants and victims of violent crimes, sexual crimes, felony or domestic crimes, coercion and exploitation attempts, or fraud Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-3222 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 988-3222 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in, call, email, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services * Crime Victims If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MULTNOMAH COUNTY COURTHOUSE
